title: Stop Letting People Play in Your Face: Knowing Your Worth with Alex Hill
summary: #336: In this week’s installment of The Business of You series, I’m sitting down with Alex Hill — recipe developer, community builder, and the woman behind Just Add Hot Sauce — to talk about what it really takes to bet on yourself as a solopreneur and what it truly means to stop letting people play in your face, including yourself. This conversation is about the moment before the leap. The panic attack that becomes the pivot. The $25K savings goal. The train that’s moving whether you’re ready or not. If you’re truly going to be the CEO of your life, it means you know your worth, and you move like it. We talk about turning content into capital, building a brand without letting the algorithm own you, and why “being the product” is both the source of opportunity and pressure. Because it’s one thing to go viral. It’s another thing to build a business that can outlive the internet. The Business of You is about ownership. Not just of your talent, but of your trajectory. This conversation is a masterclass in zooming out before the world decides your next move for you. This episode is for you if… You know you’re talented but you’re still figuring out how to think like the CEO of your own life.You want to move from scarcity to leverage.You want to learn the difference between being creative and being strategic.You’re ready to build real ownership.You want to stop performing for the algorithm.You’re learning that saying yes built the foundation but saying no builds the future.You’re tired of shaking your ass for Kohl’s cash and are ready to build real equity. title: How to Stop Undervaluing Yourself ft. Kameron Monet
summary: #334: You can’t manifest what you don’t protect. And if you’re still doing deals in DMs and hoping for the best… we need to talk. In the last episode of the How to Get What You Want series, I’m joined by attorney and tech founder Kameron Monet to get into the parts of the process we don’t romanticize enough: the structure. The contracts. The negotiation. The uncomfortable emails. Because manifestation is cute — but protection is power. Our conversation goes beyond vision boards and affirmations and into the tactical moves that actually secure the life you’re building. We talk about how to negotiate with conviction, what “perpetuity” really means (and why you should care), why you should never start work without a signed agreement, and how to stop leaving money on the table — whether you’re a creator or a corporate girl. If you want more, you need the courage to ask for it and the infrastructure to keep it. This episode is your reminder that getting what you want isn’t just about desire. title: The Hard Truth About Why Your Life Hasn’t Changed with Aycee Brown
summary: #332: If you’ve been waiting for permission, validation, or a sign to make a change, this is it. In this episode of the “How to Get What You Want” series, I’m sitting down with psychic medium, spiritual teacher, and author Aycee Brown for a conversation about the one thing standing between you and the life you want: telling yourself the truth. Because most of the time when you feel stuck, it’s a sign of self-rejection, not confusion. Together, we unpack why clarity is the real catalyst for manifestation, how many of us unconsciously abandon our desires to be more palatable, and what it looks like to trust your body, your intuition, and your inner knowing. If luck is created through alignment, then honesty is where it begins. Aycee also shares how spiritual frameworks like astrology, numerology, and human design can deepen your self-understanding, why success can feel more threatening than failure, and how small acts of self-trust build the life you’ve been envisioning. Consider this your reminder: you don’t need to become someone else to have the life you want. You need to embody who you already are.
